title: After-Action Review — February 24, 2026
scope: Add endgame clock management mechanics and fix snap kick range enforcement.

rationale.item: Eliminates the old problem where teams would awkwardly punt or run normal plays when they should be milking clock.
Discovered the old 0.001 weight floor was allowing rare snap kick selections beyond 58 yards. Replaced with a true hard-zero system.

actions.description: Added Victory Formation (kneel-down) play type letting a leading team run out the clock in Q4; burns 35-40 seconds per play, loses 1-2 yards; triggers when leading by more than 5 with under 90 seconds left, or any lead under 45 seconds.
Added defensive timeout strategy: trailing defenses call timeouts in Q4 when under 3 minutes and within 14 points; probability scales with urgency and deficit size, modified by coaching awareness.

Snap kick range fix: enforced the 58-yard cutoff after all modifiers (boot raid bonuses, take-points bias, etc.) so nothing can sneak past, replacing the 0.001 weight floor with a true hard-zero system.
Fixed the clock-management "final play" path to check actual kicker range instead of a raw field-position threshold.

risk_posture: The false positive investigation saved us from an unnecessary "fix" that would have broken legitimate blocked kick recording.
Victory formation and defensive timeouts integrated cleanly without disrupting existing play selection or scoring balance.

validation_claims.claim: 50-Game Test Suite: DK attempts >58 yards: 0 (hard zero); Max actual DK distance: 56 yards; Avg DK distance: 34.2 yards; DK attempts per game: 3.9; Kneels per game: 1.6; Avg points per team: 64.0; Score range: 29-96
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run a 50-game simulation batch and compare snap kick distance and kneel/scoring metrics.
Re-run a multi-game simulation and count kneel-down plays per game.

non_actions.item: No code change for apparent 60-84 yard DK attempts
non_actions.reason: What initially looked like 60-84 yard DK attempts were actually normal-range kicks (32-55 yards) that got blocked. The play log was recording the defense's recovery position, not where the kick was attempted from. No code change needed — it's correct behavior, just misleading if you measure from the post-play field position.

non_actions.item: No new clock-aware play selection work (suppressing kicks when leading, boosting when trailing)
non_actions.reason: was already handled by the existing 3-minute warning code. No new work needed there.

residual_risks: Kneel/timeout frequency across different team archetypes in longer dynasty sims — may want to tune thresholds if edge cases appear.
Test telemetry should always measure snap kick distance from the pre-kick field position to avoid the blocked-kick false positive issue again.
parser_notes: Date taken from the first heading ("February 24, 2026"). No branch or file paths named in the document.
